## SECURITY REVIEW: supabase-mcp-setup import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** [DEV-242](mention://issue/46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/supabase/mcp (v0.8.2)
**License:** Apache-2.0

### Install footprint
- HTTP MCP server at `https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp` — no local process
- Self-hosted option: `http://localhost:54321/mcp` (Supabase CLI)
- Requires: Supabase personal access token OR OAuth 2.1 flow
- Client-side config: `.mcp.json` entry pointing to the MCP URL

### Required scopes
- **PAT path:** Personal access token with `supabase:mcp` scope (full project access by default)
- **OAuth path:** `supabase:mcp` OAuth scope
- **Write capabilities (default, not read_only):** execute arbitrary SQL, create/drop tables, create users, manage auth, deploy edge functions
- **Read-only mode (recommended):** `?read_only=true` restricts to SELECT and read operations
- **Project scoping:** `?project_ref=<ref>` limits to one project

### Security checklist for auditor
1. **Token handling:** Confirm the skill template stores PATs only as env vars (never in `.mcp.json` or committed files)
2. **Default posture:** Verify skill enforces `read_only=true` as the documented default and requires explicit user approval to remove
3. **Scope documentation:** Confirm that write-mode removal requires workspace-owner approval per Security Rules (all write paths to external systems)
4. **Token revocation path:** Verify the skill documents how to revoke a PAT if compromised
5. **Self-hosted vs cloud:** Confirm self-hosted local MCP is lower risk than cloud MCP (no external data transmission)
6. **License compliance:** Apache-2.0 permits use in our Skill Library

### Expected outcome
- `decision=green` if skill template defaults to read-only, no PAT in config files, revocation path documented
- `decision=yellow` if write-mode default but mitigations documented (downgrade to explicit warning in skill)
- `decision=red` if PAT leakage risk or no scoping mechanism

**On green:** Set `multica issue metadata set 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 --key decision --value green`, then promote: `multica issue status 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 todo`
**On red:** Set `multica issue metadata set 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 --key blocked_reason --value security_review_failed`

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

---
*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-06-15 07:00 Asia/Kuwait. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*

issues.description:
## Input

Signal from 2026-06-14 Workspace Upgrade Report (DEV-260):
- 35 of 47 in_review issues are stale >48h. These are Daily Digest (DEV-254, DEV-252, ...) and Factory Health Audit (DEV-253, DEV-251, ...) autopilot issues.
- The quality-gate-manager (QGM) instructions contain an "Autopilot issue fast-path" that should close these automatically, but the queue shows this path is not consistently firing.
- Matching criteria A: assignee is ceo or workspace-operations-manager. Criteria B: title starts with "Daily Command Center Digest —", "Factory Health Audit —", "Daily Factory Health Audit —", or "Workspace Upgrade Report —" OR description contains "Mode=create_issue" or "Autopilot run triggered".
- Current QGM instructions say: if comment count ≥ 1 → close as done; if comment count = 0 → cancel as anomaly.
- Pattern not consistently observed: DEV-255 (Factory Health Audit 2026-06-13) has metadata={}, meaning QGM may not have run the fast-path correctly.

## Expected Output

A prompt patch to quality-gate-manager instructions that:
1. Strengthens the fast-path trigger: adds explicit examples of matching title patterns including "Daily Factory Health Audit —" (hyphen variant observed in DEV-251).
2. Adds a priority note: "Process autopilot fast-path FIRST before any standard quality gate work to prevent queue accumulation."
3. Adds a sweep clause: "On each QGM run, sweep all in_review issues — not just the one that triggered this run — and apply the fast-path to all autopilot-pattern issues found in a single pass."
4. Result: a concrete `multica agent update <qgm-id> --instructions "<patched text>"` command, or a description of the exact text delta to insert.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Patched instructions text written and reviewed for correctness
- [ ] Title pattern list is exhaustive: "Daily Command Center Digest —", "Factory Health Audit —", "Daily Factory Health Audit —", "Workspace Upgrade Report —"
- [ ] Sweep-all clause is present (not just the triggering issue)
- [ ] Priority ordering: fast-path before standard gate
- [ ] Instructions updated on quality-gate-manager via multica CLI
- [ ] Comment on this issue with: diff of changed text + expected outcome

## Dependencies

- quality-gate-manager agent ID: `d87c831d-bd4d-459f-9672-d633e238c44f`
- Read current QGM instructions first: `multica agent get d87c831d-bd4d-459f-9672-d633e238c44f --output json`
- No external blockers

## Risk

Medium. Modifying QGM instructions affects all in_review quality gates. Risk: too-broad fast-path accidentally closes real shippable work. Mitigation: fast-path only applies when BOTH criteria A (autopilot assignee) AND B (title/description pattern) match simultaneously.

## Definition of Done

- QGM instructions updated
- Fast-path sweep clause present
- Stale in_review count drops by ≥10 on the next QGM run
- Result comment on this issue

## SECURITY REVIEW: sandbox-process-runner import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** [DEV-241](mention://issue/14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime
**License:** Apache-2.0

### Install footprint
- npm package: `@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime` (global install)
- Installs `srt` CLI binary
- Wraps target process with macOS `sandbox-exec` or Linux `bubblewrap`
- Intercepts network via proxy-based filtering (man-in-the-middle for TLS inspection)
- No persistent daemon; process lifecycle tied to the sandboxed command

### Required scopes
- Root/sudo NOT required on Linux (bubblewrap is user-namespaces based)
- macOS: uses `sandbox-exec` (built-in macOS API, no elevated privileges)
- Network proxy: runs on loopback, intercepts outbound HTTP/HTTPS via LD_PRELOAD or system proxy env vars

### Security checklist for auditor
1. **Supply chain:** Confirm `@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime` is the official Anthropic package (not a typosquat)
2. **TLS proxy risk:** The package performs MITM on TLS to enforce network restrictions — verify the MITM key is ephemeral and not exported
3. **Active CVEs this week:** TLS terminate host header spoofing fix (PR #296, 2026-06-05), shell-quote bump to 1.8.4 (PR #301), leaf cert validity tightened (85a66578) — confirm all are in v0.x.y on npm
4. **Scope creep:** Verify `srt` cannot be used to escalate privileges or escape to the parent process namespace
5. **License compliance:** Apache-2.0 permits commercial use in our Skill Library

### Expected outcome
- `decision=green` if supply chain confirmed, MITM key ephemeral, no privilege escalation path
- `decision=red` if supply chain risk or unpatched CVE

**On green:** Set `multica issue metadata set 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 --key decision --value green`, then promote: `multica issue status 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 todo`
**On red:** Set `multica issue metadata set 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 --key blocked_reason --value security_review_failed`

issues.description:
## Context

Benchmark action from DEV-268: WorkspaceAgentSkillPersonalization. Replit Agent and Cursor have shipped workspace-level personalization UIs this week, letting non-technical users define reusable agent behaviors without CLI/CLAUDE.md knowledge. We need to close that gap.

## Task

Design and publish a new skill: **`user-skill-creation-wizard`**

This skill is a guided conversation flow that any workspace member (non-technical) can invoke to define a mini-skill. No CLAUDE.md authoring knowledge required.

## Required Skill Anatomy (every field is mandatory)

- **Name:** `user-skill-creation-wizard`
- **Purpose:** Guide any workspace member through a 4-step conversation to define a mini-skill, then produce a well-formed SKILL.md draft and open a Skill Library review issue.
- **Trigger:** When a workspace member wants to define a reusable agent behavior but lacks skill-authoring knowledge.
- **Required Input:** User must supply answers to 4 wizard questions (see Steps below).
- **Steps:**
  1. Ask for skill name and one-line description. Validate: name must be kebab-case, description ≤ 120 chars.
  2. Ask for trigger condition in plain language (when should the agent invoke this skill?).
  3. Ask what the agent should do: behavior, output format, constraints. Provide an example to anchor the user.
  4. Ask for optional input fields the user must supply when invoking. Offer to skip if none.
  5. Run `multica skill list` (if available) to check for naming collisions; rename if conflict found.
  6. Apply the workspace quality checklist: purpose ✓, trigger ✓, required input ✓, steps ✓, output ✓, quality checklist ✓, failure handling ✓.
  7. Generate SKILL.md content from the collected answers.
  8. Create an issue in `08 - Skill Library` (project `88c09466-8a25-419d-b28e-d3d5973197c4`) titled `[Wizard Draft] <skill-name>` with the SKILL.md as the description and status `todo`, assignee: skill-builder.
  9. Reply to the user with a link to the new issue and the generated SKILL.md preview.
- **Output:** SKILL.md file content + a new `08 - Skill Library` issue in `todo` status ready for agent review.
- **Quality checklist:** All 7 SKILL.md sections present; trigger condition is specific enough to auto-detect; steps are actionable without ambiguity; output is concrete; naming collision checked.
- **Failure handling:** If user provides vague behavior description, re-ask with a concrete example. If naming collision found, suggest 2-3 alternatives before re-asking.
- **Improvement rule:** Update when Multica adds a skill import UI or when 3+ wizard-generated skills are found to have low quality scores.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `user-skill-creation-wizard` skill file created and importable in the workspace
- [ ] Skill passes the 7-section workspace quality checklist
- [ ] Test case documented: invoke the wizard for a "weekly status email template" mini-skill and produce a ready-for-review SKILL.md
- [ ] A `08 - Skill Library` issue created for the test case output
- [ ] No hardcoded squad/assignee assumptions in the wizard (squad-agnostic)
- [ ] Skill extraction note added as a comment on DEV-268

## Definition of Done

- Skill published and importable
- At least one end-to-end test run documented (wizard invoked → SKILL.md produced → Skill Library review issue created)
- Comment posted on parent issue DEV-268 with summary and links
- No secrets exposed
issues.title: Create user-skill-creation-wizard skill

issues.description:
## Input

**Pattern observed:** WorkspaceAgentSkillPersonalization — users defining persistent custom instructions and named reusable skills at the workspace level, applied by the AI agent across all projects automatically.

**Vendors shipping this pattern this week:**
- **Replit Agent (Jun 10/12):** Workspace Settings UI lets any user write custom instructions that Agent applies globally across all projects, plus define named reusable "skills" that Agent can invoke. Ships as a self-serve settings panel — no CLAUDE.md or CLI knowledge required.
- **Cursor (Jun 11):** Auto-review profiling builds workspace-level behavioral context for agent governance, extending the pattern to risk/approval personalization.

**The gap in Multica today:** Our skill system is powerful but requires a technical agent (or the workspace owner) to author skills as structured CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md files and publish them via CLI. Non-technical workspace members cannot define or publish skills without agent mediation. Replit's version closes this gap with a UI-first flow.

**Scoring:** frequency=2/6 | impact=4 | effort=S | score=8 (highest this sweep)

---

## Expected Output

A new skill in the Skill Library (`08 - Skill Library`):

**Name:** `user-skill-creation-wizard`

**What it produces:** A guided conversation flow that any workspace member (non-technical) can invoke to define a mini-skill. The wizard collects:
1. Skill name and one-line description
2. When to use it (trigger condition, in plain language)
3. What the agent should do (behavior, output format, constraints)
4. Optional: input fields the user must supply when invoking

At the end of the conversation, the wizard generates a well-formed SKILL.md file and creates an issue in `08 - Skill Library` to formalize the skill for agent review before publishing. The wizard itself requires no CLAUDE.md knowledge from the user.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Wizard skill is published in `08 - Skill Library` and importable
- [ ] Any workspace member can invoke the wizard via a Multica issue comment or direct invocation without prior skill-authoring knowledge
- [ ] Wizard produces a SKILL.md draft that passes the workspace's skill quality checklist (purpose, trigger, required input, steps, output, quality checklist, failure handling)
- [ ] A test case: a non-technical user defines a "weekly status email template" mini-skill via the wizard, and the output SKILL.md is ready for agent review
- [ ] No hardcoded assumptions about which squad reviews the skill (the wizard should be squad-agnostic)

---

## Dependencies

- `08 - Skill Library` project must be accessible (assumed yes)
- Skill creation rules (3-times rule, SKILL.md format) from workspace CLAUDE.md must be codified as the wizard's internal rubric
- Optionally: a UI prompt for the Skill Factory Squad to review and publish the wizard's output

---

## Risk

- **Low:** Wizard generates a SKILL.md that doesn't meet the quality bar → mitigated by including the quality checklist as the wizard's final validation step before producing output
- **Medium:** Non-technical users describe vague behaviors that produce low-quality skills → mitigated by structured question flow with examples at each step
- **Low:** Skill naming collisions with existing skills → wizard should call `multica skill list` to check for duplicates before finalizing

---

## Definition of Done

- Skill published and importable
- At least one end-to-end test run documented in the skill's issue (wizard invoked → SKILL.md produced → Skill Factory review issue created)
- Skill extraction note added to this sweep issue (DEV-267)
- No secrets exposed

## Input

Signal from 2026-06-14 Workspace Upgrade Report (DEV-260):

- 35 of 47 in_review issues are stale >48h (Daily Digest, Factory Health Audit, Workspace Upgrade Report patterns).
- The `in-review-triage` skill description states: "Automatically close or escalate stale autopilot-generated issues in in_review. Targets Daily Digest, Factory Health Audit, Workspace Upgrade Report, Skill Review patterns older than 7 days."
- The quality-gate-manager has a fast-path clause for autopilot issues but it isn't clearing the stale queue fast enough.

## Expected Output

1. Assign the `in-review-triage` skill to quality-gate-manager: `multica agent skills add <quality-gate-manager-id> --skill-id 4b0bb5e9-4ec3-413d-a0bb-72f27f5f34ea`

issues.description:
Workspace Upgrade Weekly Loop — recursive workspace self-improvement. Mode=create_issue. Runs every Sunday 09:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3).

Run the `workspace-upgrade-report` skill against the live workspace and post a single ranked action list as a comment on the issue this autopilot creates. Auto-dispatch the top 3 actions to skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, and memory-curator. Never skip a week — a silent skip is itself a finding.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues except the three sub-issues for top-3 dispatch in step (6).
- NEVER mention CEO in any comment — CEO is the assignee and re-mentioning would loop (Factory Pulse v3 no-mention rule).
- NEVER mention any agent in a wrap-up. Only mention agents to delegate first-touch work on a fresh sub-issue.
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (the workspace-operations dialog).
- Read each issue's metadata before pinning anything; do not log run state. Stay within the issue this autopilot creates.

STEPS:

(1) Scope the scan window. Treat `since = now - 7 days` (RFC3339, UTC). Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

(2) Collect the five input signals:

   (a) retry_logs (last 7 days). Walk every issue updated in the past 7 days and pull metadata. Any `retry_log` key present is a failure signature. Aggregate by skill name / agent / failure_reason.
       - `multica issue list --output json --status in_progress` and `--status blocked` and `--status in_review` (paged if needed).
       - For each issue, `multica issue metadata list <id> --output json` — keep only entries with a `retry_log` key.

   (b) Blocked issues — every issue currently `status=blocked`:
       - `multica issue list --status blocked --output json`
       - For each: capture id, identifier, title, assignee, age (now - updated_at), and `blocked_reason` metadata.

   (c) Stale in_review (>48h) — every issue currently `status=in_review` whose `updated_at` is more than 48h ago:
       - `multica issue list --status in_review --output json`
       - Filter client-side by `updated_at`.

   (d) Agent WIP imbalance — for every agent in the workspace, count `in_progress` issues assigned to them. Flag any agent with WIP=0 (cold) or WIP≥4 (overloaded). Flag any squad whose total WIP ≥7.
       - `multica agent list --output json`
       - `multica issue list --status in_progress --output json` and group by `assignee_id`.

   (e) Skill orphans — every skill not assigned to any agent (`agent_count==0`), and every agent with zero skills attached.
       - `multica skill list --output json`
       - For each skill, attempt `multica skill get <id> --output json` (or inspect the skill→agent join if exposed). If the CLI exposes no join command, infer from agent definitions (`multica agent get <id> --output json` per agent) — cap the scan at 30 agents per run.

(3) Score and rank actions. For each candidate upgrade action, score on:
   - Impact (how many issues/agents this would unblock or accelerate; 1–5)
   - Effort (1=trivial, 5=multi-day)
   - Confidence (1=speculative, 5=clear pattern with ≥3 occurrences)
   Final score = (Impact × Confidence) / Effort. Surface the Top 5.

(4) Compose the upgrade report comment. Post ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue, using this exact shape so step (6) can parse it:

```
# Workspace Upgrade Report — <YYYY-MM-DD>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC)
**Signals scanned:** retry_logs=N | blocked=N | stale_in_review_48h=N | wip_outliers=N | skill_orphans=N

## Top 5 Upgrade Actions

1. **<short title>** — owner=<agent-name> | effort=<S|M|L> | impact=<1-5> | score=<n>
   - Rationale: <one sentence, cite source issue identifiers like DEV-NN>
   - Expected impact: <one sentence>

2. … (same shape)
3. … (same shape)
4. … (same shape)
5. … (same shape)

## Signal Summary
- Retry log hotspots: <skill / agent / count> …
- Stale in_review: <DEV-NN list>
- Blocked: <DEV-NN list>
- WIP outliers: <agent: count> …
- Skill orphans: <skill names>

## Next Steps
- Top 3 actions auto-dispatched to skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, memory-curator (see linked sub-issues).
- Remaining actions parked on this issue for CEO triage.
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body in). Do NOT include any `mention://agent/...` link in this comment.

(5) Pin metadata on this issue ONLY if a value is materially needed by future runs. Recommended keys: `report_window_end` (RFC3339), `top_action_count` (number, expect 5), `dispatched_action_count` (number, expect 3). Use `multica issue metadata set` once each. Skip any key whose value is identical to what you already see in `metadata list`.

(6) Auto-dispatch top 3 actions. Each top-3 action gets its own sub-issue under this autopilot's created issue, assigned to the named owner. Owner mapping rules:
   - Owner agent named in the action header (skill-builder / prompt-optimizer / memory-curator) is the assignee.
   - If the top-3 already names a different agent (e.g. action #1 is a runtime fix that belongs elsewhere), still cap dispatch at exactly three sub-issues among {skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, memory-curator}. Pick the highest-scoring action that fits each agent's domain:
       * skill-builder → new skill, skill rewrite, skill orphan adoption
       * prompt-optimizer → agent prompt edit, prompt-improvement-review target, retry_log root-cause in prompts
       * memory-curator → memory cleanup, stale-fact removal, missing-default capture
   - If no candidate fits an agent's domain this week, comment on the parent: "No skill-builder/prompt-optimizer/memory-curator action this week — backlog empty for that lane." Do NOT mint a filler sub-issue.

   - `multica issue create --title "Workspace Upgrade Action — <short title>" --project 1a1c2fb3-41ee-4b99-b820-d10f1c564052 --parent <this-issue-id> --status todo --priority high --assignee-id <agent-id> --description-stdin` with body that includes:

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.

## Skill Registration

**Multica Skill ID:** `a1468c20-4dfa-423a-a54b-4a7e82a92100`
**Name:** `universal-connector-install`
**Version:** 1.1.0 — 2026-06-08 (security review additions from DEV-227)
**Source:** Original — devosnt Skill Factory (DEV-223)

---

## Purpose

Wire integrations into any Multica-built app using the modern **connector-as-app-install** pattern. Covers:

1. **Sub-skill A — OAuth Connector Install**: Stripe, Supabase, generic REST APIs via OAuth-grant. No API key ever in generated code.
2. **Sub-skill B — MCP Server Exposure**: Expose any Multica-built app as an MCP server for Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Cursor — with explicit tool allowlist and API key auth.
3. **Sub-skill C — Connector Sidebar UX Spec**: Standard settings UI for surfacing, installing, and revoking connectors.

---

## Trigger Phrases

- "integrate Stripe / Supabase / [any third-party API]"
- "add payments", "add billing", "add subscriptions"
- "expose my app as an MCP server", "let Cursor use my app", "connect Claude Desktop to my app"
- "add a connector sidebar", "show available integrations"
- Build issue contains: *connector*, *OAuth integration*, *one-click install*, *MCP server*, *integration sidebar*

---

## Security Model

- OAuth tokens stored server-side only (never client bundle or git)
- MCP server scope is an explicit tool allowlist — no wildcard DB access
- MCP server auth via `MCP_SERVER_API_KEY` env var
- All data tools enforce RLS for multi-tenant isolation
- `secrets-leak-detector` required on every PR using this skill

---

## MCP Server Exposure — Security Requirements (Sub-skill B)

These requirements apply to every deployment generated from the MCP server exposure scaffold. They are non-negotiable and must be confirmed before any `in_review → done` transition on a build issue using this skill.

### 1. Input Validation (REQUIRED)
All MCP tool call arguments MUST be validated server-side before processing:
- Use Zod or equivalent schema validation on every tool handler
- Reject unknown/extra parameters
- Enforce maximum string lengths (default: 2048 chars unless the tool explicitly needs more)
- Validate enum values against an explicit allowlist
- Never pass raw user-supplied strings directly to DB queries or shell commands

### 2. Rate Limiting (REQUIRED — production blocker if missing)
The MCP server MUST be deployed behind a rate limiter:
- **Recommended:** max 60 tool calls/minute per API key
- **HTTP transport:** use Nginx `limit_req_zone` or equivalent reverse proxy rule
- **stdio/local deployment:** the host process (Claude Desktop, Cursor) is responsible for rate limiting; document this assumption explicitly in the app's README
- Without rate limiting, a misconfigured or abusive MCP client can exhaust server resources

### 3. Logging Policy (REQUIRED)
Log only what is needed for audit and incident response:
- **Log:** tool call name, timestamp, API key hash (first 8 characters only — never the full key)
- **Never log:** tool call arguments or return values — they may contain user PII or business-sensitive data
- Logs must be queryable by timestamp and API key hash for incident investigation
- No secrets or full tokens in any log line

### 4. HTTP Transport CORS (REQUIRED if HTTP transport is used)
The scaffold defaults to stdio transport, which has no CORS surface. If a deployment switches to HTTP transport:
- Set `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` to an explicit origin allowlist — never `*`
- Never allow `*` for credentialed requests
- Document the allowed origins in the deployment's env var config

---

## Deployment Checklist (Sub-skill B)

Before any production deployment using the MCP server exposure scaffold, confirm all of the following:

- [ ] `MCP_SERVER_API_KEY` generated via `openssl rand -hex 32` and stored in secrets manager (not in git or env file)
- [ ] `ALLOWED_TOOLS` allowlist is explicit — no wildcard entries
- [ ] All tool handlers have Zod argument validation
- [ ] Rate limiter configured (60 calls/min/key or tighter)
- [ ] Logging configured: tool name + API key hash only
- [ ] HTTPS enforced if HTTP transport is used
- [ ] `secrets-leak-detector` passed on the PR diff
- [ ] RLS policies verified for all data tools
- [ ] `npm audit` run — no high/critical CVEs

---

## Source Issue

DEV-223 — BENCHMARK ACTION: Universal MCP/connector-as-app-install pattern

## Security Review

DEV-227 — Security Review: MCP server exposure scaffold (CONDITIONAL PASS — all gaps addressed in v1.1.0)

## Improvement Rule

After 3 uses: review requested connectors and update default list. After 6 uses: extract per-connector sub-skills.

## Changes

### 1. `nextjs-supabase-builder` — added Step 7: SEO scaffold (template-builder, 2026-06-08)

New step appended to the Next.js + Supabase default scaffold. Every app built with this skill now ships with:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `app/robots.ts` | Exports crawl rules: allows `/`, disallows auth-gated routes, links to sitemap |
| `app/sitemap.ts` | Exports sitemap with home page entry; teams add new routes as they ship |
| `app/layout.tsx` | Updated with `metadata` export: title template, description, Open Graph (og:title/description/image/url), hreflang alternates (en + ar-KW) |
| `ENV_CHECKLIST.md` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` added as required Client-safe var; SEO assets section added |

Time budget updated: 6 steps → 7 steps, ≤10 min → ≤11 min (Step 7 ceiling: 30 s).

### 2. `user-stories-to-issues` — auto-injects SEO AC block for web app issues (skill-builder, 2026-06-08)

Step 3a added: any issue targeting a web app (labeled `frontend` or `full-stack`, or with routes/UI components) automatically receives the SEO Acceptance Criteria block in its AC section. No manual trigger required.

SEO AC block injected per issue:
- Semantic HTML hierarchy (`<h1>`→`<h2>`→`<h3>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<footer>`)
- Meta title ≤60 chars, unique per page
- Meta description ≤160 chars, unique per page
- Open Graph: `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:url`
- `robots.txt` with correct allow/disallow rules
- `sitemap.xml` auto-generated, public URLs only

### 3. `seo-audit-checklist` — standalone QA skill created (skill-builder, 2026-06-08)

New skill. QA runs this against any deployed web app before `in_review → done`. Covers 8 steps: meta tags, Open Graph, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, semantic HTML, canonical/hreflang, Lighthouse SEO ≥90, structured data (optional). Outputs a standardized audit report table with PASS / PASS WITH WARNINGS / FAIL verdict.

## Integration

These three skills form a complete SEO chain:

```
PRD
  → user-stories-to-issues (SEO AC injected automatically)
  → nextjs-supabase-builder (SEO scaffold files generated)
  → seo-audit-checklist (QA gate before done)
```

## Demo

[DEV-232](mention://issue/350183b7-ac4d-4db4-801c-96d215fc821c) — sample web app build issue showing the SEO AC block as auto-injected output.

- [x] `seo-audit-checklist` skill published and available to QA agents

## Atoms.dev — Competitive Brief (2026-06-01)

**Run date:** 2026-06-01 | **Window:** 2026-05-25 → 2026-06-01 | **Baseline:** DEV-62 (2026-05-26)

---

### What It Builds
Full-stack web apps and SaaS products from a natural-language prompt, using a simulated team of 8 named AI agents (Iris: research; Emma: PM; Bob: architecture; Alex: engineering; Mike: lead; Sarah: SEO; David: data; Adrian: ads). Ships with auth, PostgreSQL, Stripe, SEO, and one-click deploy. Race Mode (Max plan): four AI models build simultaneously; user picks the best result. No material product change this week; blog has had no posts since May 5, 2026.

### Onboarding Flow
Prompt → Iris does pre-build market research → 8-agent team builds concurrently → one-click deploy to Atoms Cloud. Race Mode (Max): four model builds run simultaneously. No changelog or /whats-new surface (/changelog and /whats-new both 404 as of 2026-06-01).

### Pricing (2026-06-01)
| Plan | Price | Credits/mo | Storage | Notes |
|------|-------|------------|---------|-------|
| Free | $0 | 15/day (25/mo) | 2 GB | 2 Cloud projects |
| Pro | $20/mo | 100 | 10 GB | Private projects, custom domains |
| Max | $100/mo | 500 | 100 GB | Race Mode, higher accuracy |

Team Plan (Apr 2026) absent from public pricing page — likely still in limited beta.

### Strengths
1. **Named-agent UX transparency** — 8 visible specialist agents with defined roles create user trust: "a real team is working on this." No competitor shows this. [Source: atoms.dev homepage]
2. **Pre-build research gate (Iris)** — market research, competitive analysis, and audience sizing before any code runs. 4/6 competitors now ship some form of this. [Source: atoms.dev/blog]
3. **Race Mode** — 4 parallel model iterations; user picks best result. Quality multiplier unavailable in any other platform. [Source: atoms.dev/blog]

### Weaknesses
1. **No public changelog** — /changelog and /whats-new both 404; opacity about what changed makes trust-building difficult.
2. **Race Mode gated at $100/mo** — the quality multiplier is locked behind Max tier; Pro users get no parallel-model benefit.
3. **No Arabic/RTL support found** — zero evidence of MENA market consideration.

### Patterns to Copy
- **Named-agent UX** — surface agent roles (researcher, PM, architect, engineer, QA) visibly in our build interface; builds trust without adding friction.
- **Pre-build research gate** — before starting a build, run a lightweight market/competitive research step (what exists, who uses it, what differentiates it). Iris pattern.

### Patterns to Beat
- **Arabic/RTL gap** — Atoms ships no Arabic/RTL support; our MENA-native defaults are a structural competitive advantage in the Kuwait/GCC market.
- **Changelog opacity** — our transparent, queryable issue/comment history is a trust differentiator vs Atoms' 404 changelog.

### Decision
**Study only** — Named-agent UX and pre-build research gate are worth absorbing as prompt/UX patterns; no import or rebuild warranted. Race Mode is architecturally interesting but the $100 price point and closed API limit practical adoption.

### Sources
- https://atoms.dev
- https://atoms.dev/blog (last post May 5, 2026)
- https://github.com/FoundationAgents/MetaGPT
- https://toolworthy.ai (Atoms.dev analysis)

### Diff vs 2026-05-26 Baseline
- **new_features:** No material new features this week (no blog posts since May 5)
- **pricing_changes:** —
- **new_flows:** —
- **removed:** —
- **positioning:** Hero headline changed from "world's first AI software company" (MGX era) to "Turn ideas into products that sell / AI employees to validate ideas, build products, and acquire customers" — pivoting from tech identity to founder/operator GTM; 8th agent (David, Data Analyst) now explicitly listed on homepage separately from Adrian

## Antigravity 2.0 (Google) — Competitive Brief (2026-06-01)

**Run date:** 2026-06-01 | **Window:** 2026-05-25 → 2026-06-01 | **Baseline:** DEV-62 (2026-05-26, just 7 days after GA launch)

---

### What It Builds
Google's agentic developer platform — a VS Code-forked desktop IDE plus CLI, SDK, and Managed Agents API. Handles full-stack web apps, long-running automated workflows (dependency upgrades, security scans, multi-repo refactoring), and agentic CI/CD pipelines. Dynamic subagents run frontend/backend/testing in parallel; a browser subagent (live Chrome instance) visually verifies generated UI without manual DevTools. This week: post-launch stabilization (v2.0.6, v2.0.10 patches), Linux sandboxing extended, IDE integration button added.

### Onboarding Flow
Install Antigravity desktop app (macOS/Linux/Windows) → connect GitHub repo or Google Cloud workspace → prompt agent. Manager Surface: observe multiple agents across workspaces from one interface. Scheduled tasks: cron-based background agents for dependency scans, security sweeps. IDE integration button (v2.0.6): one-click open of current workspace in Antigravity IDE.

### Pricing (2026-06-01)
| Plan | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Free (Individuals) | $0 | 20 req/day (slashed from 250); full feature access |
| AI Pro (bundled) | ~$20/mo | Google AI Pro subscription |
| AI Ultra | $100/mo | 5× Pro limits |
| AI Ultra Premium | $200/mo | 20× Pro limits (was $250) |
| Enterprise | Custom | VPC, SSO, audit logs, BigQuery |

**Key change:** Free tier slashed from 250 → 20 req/day. $100 bonus credits promo expired May 25.

### Strengths
1. **Parallel dynamic subagents** — frontend/backend/testing agents run concurrently by default; A2A protocol enables inter-agent communication. Only platform in cohort to architect this at the OS level. [Source: google.com/antigravity, Google I/O 2026]
2. **Scheduled cron agents** — agents run background jobs on schedule (dependency scans, security sweeps) without user prompts. No competitor ships this as a first-class feature. [Source: Antigravity 2.0 docs]
3. **Google ecosystem depth** — native Firebase, Android, AI Studio, Google Cloud integrations; enterprise VPC/SSO/audit via Managed Agents API. [Source: Google I/O 2026 blog]

### Weaknesses
1. **Free tier gutted** — 250 → 20 req/day is a 92% reduction; makes experimentation impractical without paid plan.
2. **Post-launch stability** — v2.0.2–v2.0.9 were emergency patches (config wipes, data loss, SSH/WSL breakage from automatic upgrade); trust damage is real.
3. **No built-in deploy** — requires existing infra; contrast Lovable/Bolt one-click deploy.

### Patterns to Copy
- **Scheduled cron agents** — agents run on schedule for routine maintenance (dependency checks, security scans, changelog summaries). Directly applicable to our weekly sweep autopilot and QA automation.
- **A2A inter-agent protocol** — typed message contracts between agents enable handoff without free-text context passing.

### Patterns to Beat
- **Free tier gutted** — our accessible pricing (or volume-based model) is a competitive wedge for individual developers and small teams.
- **Desktop-only** — our browser-accessible agents vs Antigravity's install-required approach.

### Decision
**Study only** — Parallel subagent orchestration and scheduled cron agent patterns are architecturally worth studying; A2A protocol is directly applicable to our squad handoff model. Do not recommend Antigravity to clients until MCP auth fragmentation and the slashed free tier are addressed.

### Sources
- https://antigravity.google (or current URL)
- https://developers.google.com/antigravity (changelog)
- https://techcrunch.com (Google I/O 2026 coverage, May 19 2026)
- https://gradually.ai (Antigravity changelog tracker)

### Diff vs 2026-05-26 Baseline
- **new_features:** v2.0.6 IDE integration button (one-click open workspace in Antigravity IDE, May 22); v2.0.10 G1 credit bug fix + reliability (May 28); Linux sandboxing extended (previously macOS-only)
- **pricing_changes:** Free tier slashed 250 → 20 req/day; $100 bonus credits promo expired May 25
- **new_flows:** IDE integration button reduces context-switch friction
- **removed:** —
- **positioning:** Unchanged (professional developer platform, Google ecosystem depth)

## SKILL IMPORT: supabase-mcp-setup

**Source:** https://github.com/supabase/mcp (v0.8.2, released 2026-06-05)
**License:** Apache-2.0
**Stars:** 2725
**Maintained by:** Supabase (official)

### What it does
Official Supabase MCP server. Connects AI assistants (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.) directly to Supabase projects for:
- Database operations (query, schema inspection, migrations)
- Auth user management
- Edge Function deployment and logs
- Storage bucket operations
- Realtime subscription inspection

Supports `read_only` mode (recommended by default), project scoping via `project_ref`, and OAuth 2.1 or personal access token auth.

**New in v0.8.2:** Repo URL updates after transfer from `supabase/mcp-server-supabase` to `supabase/mcp`.

### Proposed Multica skill name
`supabase-mcp-setup`

### PR-ready spec
- **Purpose:** Configure and validate the Supabase MCP server so AI agents can operate directly on Supabase databases with least-privilege access
- **Trigger:** Setting up AI assistant integration with a Supabase project; onboarding a new app that uses Supabase
- **Required input:** Supabase project ref (or self-hosted URL), access token type (PAT or OAuth)
- **Steps:**
  1. Add MCP config block to `.mcp.json` — use `read_only=true&project_ref=<ref>` query params
  2. Generate PAT in Supabase dashboard → account → access tokens, OR use OAuth flow (`https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp`)
  3. Verify connection with a safe read-only query (e.g. list tables)
  4. For self-hosted: point URL to `http://localhost:54321/mcp`
  5. Document scope (read-only vs read-write, project ref) in issue metadata
- **Output:** Working `.mcp.json` entry; connection verified; scope documented
- **Quality checklist:** read_only=true unless write access explicitly approved; project_ref scoped to single project; no PAT committed to git; self-hosted and cloud variants both documented

### Security considerations (for security-auditor review)
- Source URL: https://github.com/supabase/mcp
- Install footprint: HTTP MCP server at `https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp`; no local process
- Required scopes: `supabase:mcp` OAuth scope or a personal access token with database/project access
- Write risk: in default mode can execute arbitrary SQL, create/drop tables, manage auth users
- Mitigations: `read_only=true` query param restricts to SELECT; `project_ref` scopes to single project; `--allowed-tools` can further restrict tools

### Acceptance Criteria
- SKILL.md with correct Multica frontmatter
- Both cloud and self-hosted Supabase setup paths documented
- Default config template uses `read_only=true`
- Security note: "Obtain written approval before removing read_only mode in production"
- Integration with existing `self-hosted-supabase-tunnel` skill noted

### Dependencies
- **Security review sub-issue must reach `done` with `decision=green` before this flips to `todo`** — this one has the highest scope risk of the three

### Risk
High — grants AI agents direct DB access; explicit write-mode requires production-write approval per workspace security rules

### Definition of Done
- SKILL.md written and merged to Skill Library project
- Skill visible in `multica skill list`
- Security review green stamp attached

## Replit Agent — Competitive Brief (2026-06-01)

**Run date:** 2026-06-01 | **Window:** 2026-05-25 → 2026-06-01 | **Baseline:** DEV-62 (2026-05-26)

---

### What It Builds
Full-stack web and mobile apps from natural language in a cloud IDE, with autonomous 200-min runs and real-browser self-testing. Agent 4 (2026) runs parallel artifact builds. This week shipped five new generation modalities: audio (music/SFX/speech), 3D models (Tripo3D), and Canvas-native media (images, vectors, videos). Also added Stripe one-click install from Marketplace (no API key copying required). Pivoting hard toward enterprise: Visa investment, $9B valuation, Solution Partner Program launched.

### Onboarding Flow
Prompt → live URL in <2 minutes. Plan-while-build mode (Agent 4): agent shows execution plan concurrently as it builds. Stripe Marketplace (May 29): one-click install, no API key copying. Design Canvas: Figma import → static site in <2 min. 30+ connectors platform.

### Pricing (2026-06-01)
| Plan | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Starter | $0 | Limited |
| Core | $20/mo (annual) | $20 credits included |
| Pro | $95/mo (annual) | 15 builders |
| Enterprise | Custom | SLA, SSO |

No changes from May 26 baseline.

### Strengths
1. **Five new generation modalities (May 29)** — Agent audio generation (music/SFX/speech), Tripo3D connector (3D from text/image), Canvas media generation (images/vectors/videos), Stripe one-click Marketplace install, Enterprise self-serve up to $200K. [Source: blog.replit.com, May 29 2026]
2. **Enterprise pivot signals** — Visa investment (May 28), Solution Partner Program (Accenture, Slalom, Hexaware); valuation $3B (Sep 2025) → $9B (Mar 2026); fastest-growing enterprise trajectory in this cohort. [Source: blog.replit.com]
3. **Connector-as-app-install pattern (Stripe Marketplace)** — one-click install eliminates API key copying friction; immediately applicable to any connector integration. [Source: blog.replit.com, May 29]

### Weaknesses
1. **Credit opacity** — effort-based pricing model; large builds are unpredictable in cost.
2. **Cloud-only** — no local/offline mode; existing repos with complex Git workflows are awkward.

### Patterns to Beat

- **Cloud-only** — our repo-native stack runs anywhere; Replit requires cloud.

### Decision
**Adapt** — The connector-as-app-install pattern (Stripe Marketplace one-click) is immediately importable as an integration scaffolding improvement (S effort, high impact for client-facing app builds). Plan-while-build is worth adding to our build agent UX.

## SKILL IMPORT: sandbox-process-runner

**Source:** https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime
**License:** Apache-2.0
**Stars:** 4345
**Maintained by:** Anthropic (experimental)

### What it does
OS-level sandboxing for AI agents and MCP servers. Uses `sandbox-exec` on macOS and `bubblewrap` on Linux to enforce filesystem and network restrictions on arbitrary processes without requiring Docker/containers.

Key capabilities:
- Network restrictions: allowlist/denylist by domain, blocks by default
- Filesystem restrictions: control read/write per path
- Unix socket restrictions: control local IPC access
- Violation monitoring: real-time violation log on macOS
- MCP server sandboxing: wrap any `npx` MCP server with `srt` prefix

Installation: `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime`

### Proposed Multica skill name
`sandbox-process-runner`

### PR-ready spec
- **Purpose:** Wrap agent subprocesses and MCP servers in OS-level sandbox with minimal filesystem+network footprint
- **Trigger:** Spawning an MCP server, external CLI tool, or agent subprocess that needs blast-radius containment; security review asks for process isolation
- **Required input:** Process command to sandbox, list of allowed write paths, list of allowed domains (empty = block all network)
- **Steps:**
  1. `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime` (check `srt --version` to skip if already installed)
  2. Create `~/.srt-settings.json` with `filesystem.allowWrite`, `network.allowedDomains` from input
  3. Prefix the process command: `srt <original-command>`
  4. Verify with `--dry-run` first; on macOS, tail violation log (`/var/log/com.apple.sandbox.reporting.db` or `srt monitor`)
  5. Document the allowlist as a comment in the issue or PR
- **Output:** Updated `.mcp.json` or shell invocation with `srt` prefix + annotated `~/.srt-settings.json`
- **Quality checklist:** Dry-run passes; filesystem deny-write confirmed on sensitive paths; network block confirmed on unexpected domains; violation log shows only expected events

### Acceptance Criteria
- SKILL.md with correct Multica frontmatter
- Concrete example: sandboxing a filesystem MCP server (from sandbox-runtime README)
- macOS and Linux variants documented (sandbox-exec vs bubblewrap)
- Install check idempotent (skip if already installed)
- Security notes section: what SRT does NOT protect (kernel exploits, same-user processes)

### Dependencies
- Security review sub-issue (DEV-TBD) must reach `done` with `decision=green` before this flips to `todo`

### Risk
Medium — installs a global npm package from Anthropic; active security work in upstream (TLS bypass fixed this week)

### Definition of Done
- SKILL.md written and merged to Skill Library project
- Skill visible in `multica skill list`
- Example `.mcp.json` with `srt` prefix tested end-to-end

## Input

**Pattern observed:** Lovable shipped Skills (May 18, GA in June 2026) — reusable markdown-based instruction playbooks that are slash-command invokable or auto-applied when relevant. 3 of 6 benchmarked competitors (Lovable, Cursor, Atoms) now ship some form of reusable instruction system. Lovable's implementation is cleanest: markdown files stored in workspace, /slash-command invocation, 5 built-in skills (/redesign, /accessibility, /seo-review, /movie-creator, /skill-creator), shareable via GitHub/ZIP.

**Source:** https://lovable.dev/blog/introducing-skills | https://docs.lovable.dev/features/skills | Benchmark sweep DEV-218 (2026-06-08), score=15.0 (frequency=3/6, impact=5, effort=S)

**Our current state:** We have a Skill Library (project 08) with skills defined as issues. Build agents do not currently accept /skill-name syntax in task prompts. Skills are invoked by the platform based on agent configuration, not inline by the user at task-time.

## Expected Output

A new Multica skill `slash-skill-invoker` that:
1. Allows a user (or another agent) to prefix a build task prompt with `/skill-name` (e.g. `/accessibility Build a login form`)
2. Resolves `/skill-name` to the matching skill in the workspace Skill Library (project 08)
3. Injects the skill's full instruction content into the build agent's task context before execution
4. Ships 5 built-in starter skills: `/redesign`, `/accessibility`, `/seo-review`, `/qa-check`, `/security-scan`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Skill is published in project 08 - Skill Library
- [ ] Trigger: any agent task prompt starting with `/skill-name` activates the invoker
- [ ] Resolution: skill name matches an issue title in project 08 (case-insensitive, partial match allowed)
- [ ] Injection: resolved skill instructions prepended to the build agent's context before first tool call
- [ ] 5 built-in starter skills documented with SKILL.md format
- [ ] Fallback: if no matching skill found, agent proceeds normally and posts "Skill `/name` not found — continuing without it."
- [ ] Quality: tested on at least 2 real build tasks with /seo-review and /qa-check

## Dependencies

- Project 08 - Skill Library must be accessible
- Build agent(s) that will consume the injected context must be identified

## Risk

- Low: Skill resolution is a prompt-injection step with no side effects if skill is not found
- Medium: Skill instructions may conflict with task-specific context; need conflict-resolution rule (skill wins on format; task prompt wins on scope)

## Definition of Done

- Skill published in project 08
- At least 2 successful end-to-end test runs documented as comments on this issue
- 5 built-in starter skills available in project 08
- No regressions on existing build agent behavior
issues.title: BENCHMARK ACTION: Slash-skill-invoker — reusable instruction playbooks for build agents

## Rationale
ADR-001 (DEV-128) documents the default stack for web apps (Next.js + Supabase + Vercel) but makes no decision on the mobile layer. The Android Attendance app was built using Expo (React Native) with a local apk build path, but:
1. No ADR formalizes Expo as the default mobile stack
2. The android-apk-local-builder skill has retry_log=3 — three consecutive QA gate failures (DEV-174)
3. EAS Build (Expo's cloud CI) is deferred per local-first policy but not formally documented
4. There is no guidance on when to use React Native vs. a web PWA vs. a native Android/Kotlin app

Without a formal mobile layer decision, future app requests (e.g. HR mobile, delivery driver app) will diverge in stack choice.

## Evidence
- ADR-001 (DEV-128): no mobile layer defined
- DEV-43 (Android Attendance build): Expo + local Gradle build
- DEV-174 (Workspace Upgrade Action): android-apk-local-builder skill retry_log=3, in_review
- DEV-30 (Architecture: Android Attendance): Expo/React Native used without a formal ADR backing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR-001 updated (or an ADR-003 created) with a mobile layer section: default=Expo (React Native), build=EAS Build for production / local Gradle for dev, when to deviate (native-only requirement, AR/VR, heavy OS integration)
- [ ] Minimum viable mobile build checklist documented (env vars, signing, EAS profile)
- [ ] android-apk-local-builder skill fix (DEV-174) referenced as the validation path

## Definition of Done
- Mobile layer decision is in a signed ADR
- Solution Architect and CEO Command Squad have reviewed
